creator: neldredge@ucsd.edu
text:
Ports seem to lack dependencies on the shared libraries they're linked
with.  gqview is an example.

vulcan# pkg_info -v gqview-2.0.4_1 |grep "^Dependency"
Dependency: pkg-config-0.22
Dependency: perl-5.8.8
Dependency: libiconv-1.9.2_2
Dependency: glib-2.12.13
Dependency: gettext-0.16.1_3
Dependency: atk-1.18.0_1
vulcan# ldd `which gqview`
/usr/local/bin/gqview:
        libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libgtk-x11-2.0.so.0 (0x8006e2000)
        libgdk-x11-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libgdk-x11-2.0.so.0 (0x800b69000)
        libatk-1.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libatk-1.0.so.0 (0x800cfe000)
        libgdk_pixbuf-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libgdk_pixbuf-2.0.so.0 (0x800e1
e000)
        libpangocairo-1.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libpangocairo-1.0.so.0 (0x800f3
5000)
        libXext.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXext.so.6 (0x80103f000)
        libXrender.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libXrender.so.1 (0x801150000)
        libXinerama.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libXinerama.so.1 (0x801259000)
        libXi.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXi.so.6 (0x80135b000)
        libXrandr.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libXrandr.so.2 (0x801463000)
        libXcursor.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libXcursor.so.1 (0x80156a000)
        libXfixes.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libXfixes.so.3 (0x801674000)
        libcairo.so.2 => /usr/local/lib/libcairo.so.2 (0x801779000)
        libpangoft2-1.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libpangoft2-1.0.so.0 (0x8018f3000
)
        libfontconfig.so.1 => /usr/local/lib/libfontconfig.so.1 (0x801a23000)
        libfreetype.so.9 => /usr/local/lib/libfreetype.so.9 (0x801b56000)
        libz.so.3 => /lib/libz.so.3 (0x801ccc000)
        libpango-1.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libpango-1.0.so.0 (0x801de0000)
        libm.so.4 => /lib/libm.so.4 (0x801f23000)
        libX11.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libX11.so.6 (0x80203f000)
        libgobject-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libgobject-2.0.so.0 (0x802246000)
        libgmodule-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libgmodule-2.0.so.0 (0x802385000)
        libglib-2.0.so.0 => /usr/local/lib/libglib-2.0.so.0 (0x802488000)
        libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x802621000)
        libintl.so.8 => /usr/local/lib/libintl.so.8 (0x802812000)
        libpthread.so.2 => /lib/libpthread.so.2 (0x80291b000)
        libc.so.6 => /lib/libc.so.6 (0x802a46000)
        libXau.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXau.so.6 (0x802c55000)
        libpng.so.5 => /usr/local/lib/libpng.so.5 (0x802d58000)
        libexpat.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libexpat.so.6 (0x802e7e000)
        libXdmcp.so.6 => /usr/local/lib/libXdmcp.so.6 (0x802fa0000)
        librpcsvc.so.3 => /usr/lib/librpcsvc.so.3 (0x8030a5000)

Note, among others, the lack of a dependency on png.  This despite the
appearance of LIB_DEPENDS=png.5:${PORTSDIR}/graphics/png in
/usr/ports/graphics/gqview/Makefile.

gqview was installed with "make install" in /usr/ports/graphics/gqview.

This seems to be a regression, since before reinstalling gqview it had
way more dependencies.

vulcan# grep "^Dependency" /tmp/gqview-deps.old 
Dependency: pkg-config-0.22

Dependency: trapproto-3.4.3

Dependency: fontconfig-2.4.2_2,1

Dependency: libXprintAppUtil-1.0.1

Dependency: encodings-1.0.2,1

Dependency: font-misc-meltho-1.0.0_1

Dependency: libXcomposite-0.3.2,1
Dependency: font-bh-ttf-1.0.0

Dependency: pango-1.16.5
Dependency: gtk-2.10.14

I originally noticed this with mplayer, which quit working due to
missing shared libraries after a session with pkg_cutleaves.  firefox
did, too.  I don't offhand know when this broke, but I do know that
pkg_cutleaves was fine the last time I ran it, which was probably a
few months ago.  Well after the release of 6.2 anyway.
